use_when: The Lab must test whether a bridge between formal models survives observable contracts.
The value is separating physical invariants from finite-size or representation artifacts.
do_not_use_when: The request only asks for narrative analogy between theories.
No measurable observable, null model or reproducible sweep can be declared.

starter_observables.name: spectral_gap
starter_observables.risk: Can be dominated by unfolding or scale choice.

starter_observables.name: participation_entropy
starter_observables.risk: Finite-size regimes can masquerade as asymptotic physics.

starter_observables.name: bridge_frequency
starter_observables.role: How often a candidate bridge appears across sweeps.
starter_observables.risk: Graph-only frequency is not physical evidence.

starter_observables.name: classical_intermediate
starter_observables.role: Checks whether a quantum/formal bridge crosses a classical control.
starter_observables.risk: Can be an artifact of projection.

starter_baselines.name: label_shuffle
starter_baselines.role: Attacks semantic labels detached from structure.

starter_falsifiers.blocks: Promoting a boundary that moves with size.
Calling a result invariant when it depends on the measuring ruler.
Treating graph topology as physical proof.

admin_boundaries.boundary: Run current sweep contract without forcing the next theory target.
Promotion requires observable contract and independent sweep survival.

e2e_checks.expectation: A completed cycle updates bridge candidates with null and finite-size status.

adaptation_questions: Which physical system supplies the observable contract?
Which representation changes must the result survive?
Which nulls attack the candidate bridge first?
